us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic developments from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Maine Democrats coalesce around Troy Jackson for crucial Senate race
bullets: Troy Jackson emerged as the leading Democratic replacement candidate in Maine’s Senate race.
Several rivals dropped out and endorsed Jackson ahead of a July 25 nominating convention.
The seat is viewed as central to Democrats’ effort to win Senate control in November.

articles.title: Trump administration revives dormant deportation court for ‘alien terrorist’ cases
bullets: The administration activated a rarely used legal mechanism tied to alleged terrorism-related deportations.
The move appeared in Monday’s US politics live coverage as a key domestic policy development.
Immigration enforcement remains a central issue in the midterm-year political fight.

articles.title: Scientists organize against Trump proposal to overhaul $1T grant system
bullets: Researchers criticized an OMB proposal that would restructure federal grant-making.
Scientists warned the plan would shift large funding decisions toward political appointees.
The dispute centers on research funding, academic independence and federal science priorities.

articles.title: New York faces renewed push to ban Central Park horse carriages
bullets: Animal advocates are pressing again to end Central Park’s horse-carriage trade.
The campaign gained momentum after a tourist’s death heightened scrutiny of the industry.
Supporters of the carriage trade argue it provides jobs and that horses are treated well.

articles.title: Minnesota House candidate emphasizes affordability in congressional bid
bullets: High school math teacher Jake Johnson is campaigning for a US House seat in Minnesota.
His message centers on affordability and economic pressures facing families.
The race is part of the broader 2026 midterm battle for control of Congress.

articles.title: Ukraine targets Moscow with drones after major Russian attack on Kyiv
bullets: Ukraine launched drone attacks toward Moscow after one of Russia’s largest strikes on Kyiv.
Russian attacks also hit Odesa, where local officials reported deaths and injuries.
The escalation came amid political pressure inside Ukraine over military leadership.

articles.title: US identifies soldiers killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
bullets: The US military identified two soldiers killed in an Iranian attack in Jordan.
The deaths came as US strikes on Iran continued for a ninth night.
Maritime authorities also reported a vessel fire in the Strait of Hormuz area.

articles.title: Andy Burnham takes office as UK prime minister
bullets: Andy Burnham was appointed UK prime minister after Keir Starmer formally resigned.
Burnham used his first speech to promise a new economic direction and action on rough sleeping.
He said his government would announce cost-of-living plans on Tuesday.

articles.title: Large search operation follows passenger ferry sinking off Guyana
bullets: Rescue teams launched a major search after a passenger ferry sank off Guyana’s coast.
Authorities were looking for missing passengers after the maritime disaster.
The incident drew regional attention as emergency crews continued operations.

articles.title: Hungary’s Péter Magyar taps chess great Judit Polgár for presidency
bullets: Hungarian prime minister Péter Magyar selected chess legend Judit Polgár for president.
Magyar said Hungary needed a figure the whole nation could be proud of.
The move followed constitutional changes affecting the presidential mandate.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India stories from today and the weekend

articles.title: Police use tear gas to stop Cockroach movement march on Parliament
bullets: Police used tear gas and batons against Cockroach movement supporters in New Delhi.
Protesters demanded the education minister’s resignation over exam-paper leak allegations.
The march coincided with the opening of Parliament’s Monsoon Session.

articles.title: Sonam Wangchuk hospitalized after 20-day hunger strike
bullets: Delhi police moved activist Sonam Wangchuk to a hospital over health concerns.
Wangchuk had been hunger-striking in support of education reform and protest demands.
His hospitalization galvanized supporters ahead of the Parliament march.

articles.title: Monsoon fury kills 17 across India, with J&K worst hit
bullets: Rain-related incidents killed 17 people across India, according to the report.
Jammu and Kashmir accounted for 12 of the deaths amid floods and landslides.
Heavy-rain alerts were triggered in several states.

articles.title: Lok Sabha adjourned amid opposition uproar over NEET and Ram Mandir row
bullets: Lok Sabha proceedings were disrupted soon after the Monsoon Session began.
Opposition members raised the NEET controversy and alleged Ram Mandir donation theft.
The confrontation signaled a turbulent parliamentary session.

articles.title: PV Sindhu wins Japan Open, ending major title drought
bullets: PV Sindhu defeated Akane Yamaguchi 21-17, 21-17 to win the Japan Open.
The title was her biggest since the 2019 World Championships crown.
The win boosted Indian hopes ahead of the Delhi World Championships.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Market movers and stock stories investors are watching

articles.title: Nvidia and chip stocks remain pressured as AI-trade concerns persist
bullets: Nvidia weighed on the market as chip stocks sold off late last week.
Investor concern centered on whether AI spending can keep supporting elevated valuations.
The broader Nasdaq fell sharply in the latest full trading session.

articles.title: Netflix shares slump after mixed earnings and growth concerns
bullets: Netflix stock fell sharply after its second-quarter earnings report.
Revenue and guidance renewed investor concerns about future growth.
The company also disclosed expanding use of generative AI across titles.

articles.title: IBM extends slump after preliminary Q2 results miss expectations
bullets: IBM shares plunged after preliminary second-quarter results came in below Wall Street forecasts.
The company said customers were shifting spending toward AI infrastructure.
IBM’s full earnings report is scheduled for later this week.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs pulls back after earnings-driven record run
bullets: Goldman Sachs had surged after second-quarter profit beat expectations.
Trading strength and a corporate dealmaking rebound helped results.
Shares later moved lower as investors reassessed the run-up.

articles.title: Tesla slips as investors track robotaxi regulation debate
bullets: Tesla was among names watched in the debate over autonomous-vehicle rules.
A San Francisco hearing drew Tesla, Uber, Lyft and Waymo representatives.
The regulatory fight is shaping sentiment around robotaxi growth plans.

articles.title: EU fines AliExpress a record €550M under Digital Services Act
bullets: The European Commission fined AliExpress €550 million over illegal and fake goods.
The penalty is the largest imposed under the EU’s Digital Services Act.
Regulators said large platforms must identify and address consumer-safety risks.

articles.title: Ryanair says planes are safe after passenger window incident
bullets: Ryanair defended its aircraft safety after a passenger was nearly sucked out of a window.
The airline faced scrutiny following the onboard incident.
The company said its planes remained safe to operate.

articles.title: Bezos and UK government back £2B AI materials startup CuspAI
bullets: CuspAI raised $450 million in funding from investors including Jeff Bezos and the UK sovereign AI fund.
The Cambridge startup was valued at $2.6 billion after the round.
Its AI Materials Foundry aims to speed discovery of materials for chips and other industries.

articles.title: Databricks reaches $188B valuation amid AI data-platform boom
bullets: Databricks hit a $188 billion valuation in its latest funding-linked update.
The company is benefiting from enterprise demand for AI data infrastructure.
The valuation extends its position as one of AI’s biggest private-company stories.

articles.title: Valar Atomics seeks new funding at $6B valuation
bullets: Nuclear startup Valar Atomics is in talks to raise new capital.
The proposed round would value the company at about $6 billion.
The fundraising reflects investor interest in energy sources for AI and industrial demand.

tech.label: Tech
tech.subtitle: Technology and AI stories to know

articles.title: Netflix discloses $587M price for Ben Affleck’s AI filmmaking startup
bullets: Netflix said it paid $587 million in cash for InterPositive.
The AI filmmaking startup was co-founded by Ben Affleck.
Netflix said around 300 titles have already used generative AI.

articles.title: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang visit puts Japan AI chip strategy in focus
bullets: TechCrunch outlined key questions after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit.
Japan’s AI ambitions are tied to access to advanced Nvidia chips and domestic model efforts.
The trip highlighted Japan’s push to strengthen AI infrastructure.

articles.title: Apple lawsuit threatens to complicate OpenAI hardware plans
bullets: Apple’s legal action has become a new obstacle for OpenAI’s hardware ambitions.
The dispute comes as OpenAI works on AI device plans tied to Jony Ive’s design group.
The case adds legal uncertainty around OpenAI’s consumer-hardware strategy.

articles.title: Current AI races to build a free ‘World Wide Web of AI’
bullets: Nonprofit Current AI is building shared AI infrastructure intended to be open and accessible.
The project aims to make AI resources available beyond major private labs.
Its backers frame the effort as a public-interest counterweight in the AI race.

articles.title: Waymo resumes San Francisco service after brief pause
bullets: Waymo said its San Francisco service resumed after a one-hour pause.
The pause occurred amid a local power-outage disruption.
The incident was another operational test for urban robotaxi networks.
